msgs.text:
Turn 6 — degree 9 dies, and odd residual layers are formal boundaries The target is still the over-G isomorphism problem for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from the relators rS,rH, with F=P⋊G and P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1.The previous turns had already reduced every surviving obstruction to the residual Ef-piece, i.e. to the completed mod-2, s=1 Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q with X=1+A, Y=1+B, and target X2=1+A2.

Turn 5 gave explicit Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8)satisfyingS(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))≡X2 (modI9),and noted that the first remaining tail for that witness begins in degree 9.

The new result is:The degree-9 residual obstruction also dies.More importantly, degree 9 is not a special accident: every odd residual layer is automatically killable once the preceding layer has been solved. Thus the next genuinely dangerous layer is degree 10, not degree 9.LetG8:=S(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))X−2.

Turn 5 saysG8=1+Δ9+O(I10).

A direct truncated Magnus expansion gives ∣supp(Δ9)∣=250.That support size looks large, but it is misleading. Since G8 is group-like in the mod-2 completed tensor Hopf algebra, the first nonzero homogeneous term Δ9 is primitive. HenceΔ9∈L9,where L∙ is the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B inside F2⟨A,B⟩.Now comes the structural point. For an odd degree n, there is no new restricted-square generator in Ln. ThereforeLn=[A,Ln−1]+[B,Ln−1],where in characteristic 2,[U,V]=UV+VU.So every odd-degree primitive tail is automatically a boundary for the correction complex.

Concretely, if K=1+W+O(In) is a group element with W∈Ln−1, then the residual linearization givesS(Y0,Y1

K,q)−S(Y0,Y1,q)≡AW+WA(modIn+1),and similarlyS(Y0,Y1,qK)−S(Y0,Y1,q)≡BW+WB(modIn+1).

The same BW+WB formula holds if the correction is placed on Y0. This is exactly the degree-n boundary map∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln,(W,Q)↦AW+WA+BQ+QB.

For n=9, the computation is:dimL8=36,dimL9=56,and the mapL8⊕L8→L9,(W,Q)↦[A,W]+[B,Q]has rank 56. Thus it is onto L9, and the degree-9 tail must be killable.One explicit certificate is as follows. Put

C=(X,Y),D=(X,C),E=(X,Y2).

There are degree-8 restricted representatives P1,Pq such thatY0(9):=Y0(8),Y1(9):=Y1(8)P1,q(9):=q(8)Pq satisfyS(Y0(9),Y1(9),q(9))≡X2 (modI10).

One valid choice is obtained by taking P1 to be the product of the following degree-8 factors:P1=C4 ⋅(X,(X,E2))⋅(X,(X,(X,(X,C2))))⋅(X,(X,(X,(X,(X,E)))))⋅(X,(X,(Y,(X,(X,E)))))⋅(X,(X,(Y,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(X,(Y,(X,(Y,C2))))⋅(X,(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))))⋅(Y,(X,(X,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(Y,(X,(Y,(X,Y4)))).

And Pq may be taken asPq=(X,(X,C))2 ⋅(Y,E)2 ⋅(X,(X,(X,(X,C2))))⋅(X,(X,(Y,(X,Y4))))⋅(X,(X,(Y,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(X,(Y,E2))⋅(X,(Y,D2))⋅(X,(Y,(X,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(X,(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))))⋅(Y,(X,E2))⋅(Y,(X,D2))⋅(Y,(X,(X,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(Y,(X,(Y,(X,Y4))))⋅(Y,(X,(Y,(X,(Y,E)))))⋅(Y,(Y,E2)).

All factors in P1 and Pq begin in Magnus degree 8, so they do not disturb the congruence already achieved modulo I9. Their degree-9 linear effect is exactly [A,in8(P1)]+[B,in8(Pq)],which equals the computed Δ9. Hence the corrected residual lift satisfiesS(Y0(9),Y1(9),q(9))=X2+O(I10).This strengthens Turn 5 from “cleared through homogeneous degree 8” to “cleared through homogeneous degree 9.” It also changes the strategy: do not spend future effort hunting odd-degree tails. Once an even layer has been cleared, the following odd layer is killed formally by the primitive-boundary argument above.

ESTABLISHED: The residual Ef, mod-2, s=1, section-twisted Magnus obstruction vanishes through degree 9:S(Y0(9),Y1(9),q(9))≡X2 (modI10).More generally, every odd residual obstruction layer is a formal boundary in [A,Ln−1]+[B,Ln−1] once the preceding layer has been solved.

GAP: This still does not prove ΓS≅GΓH. The next possible genuine obstruction is degree 10, where restricted-square/Bockstein terms can appear and where nonlinear interactions such as 4+6 and 5+5 must be included.

NEXT: Build the degree-10 residual Maurer–Cartan equation, including square terms from L5[2], interactions among lower corrections, and the affine freedom left by the degree-8 and degree-9 choices.[DEG9-CLEARED-ODD-LAYERS-FORMAL]
